scope: Load-bearing economic and runtime-safety claims in docs/*.architecture.en.md. This inventory is the source dataset for future semantic drift auditing. Absolute-verb hygiene for architecture prose is enforced alongside the inventory.

claims.claim: Axial Router selects among candidate routes by maximum recipient expected_output only; price_impact and total_fees are informational quote fields, not selection inputs.
claims.falsification_note: Reintroducing a non-output selection score or preferring TMC/XYK by policy would make max-output selection tests fail or diverge from prepare_optimal_route behavior.

claims.claim: Pre-swap EMA snapshot and deviation guards on direct routes are shipped mitigations, not flash-loan or sandwich immunity; multi-hop routes rely on slippage only.
claims.falsification_note: Removing direct-route EMA validation lets over-threshold trades succeed; architecture prose claiming immunity would also fail absolute-verb hygiene.

claims.claim: Integral-based minting computes the mathematical cost/output for the curve instead of using ad-hoc linear shortcuts.
claims.falsification_note: Changing zero-slope proportional minting or the quadratic/integral output path breaks fixed expected-output assertions.

claims.claim: TMC mint calculations use U256 intermediate arithmetic so large inputs fail safely instead of overflowing silently.
claims.falsification_note: Replacing checked U256 arithmetic with overflowing u128 math causes large-input tests to panic or return an unsafe non-error result.

claims.claim: Direct Axial Router routes are bounded by slippage plus EMA price-deviation checks.
claims.falsification_note: Removing direct-route EMA validation or changing the threshold math lets the over-threshold pool-depth case execute instead of failing with PriceDeviationExceeded.

claims.claim: A same-market buy/sell round trip is net-negative due to router fees plus AMM curvature; this is not a complete MEV/sandwich-resistance guarantee.
claims.falsification_note: Removing router fees and/or changing XYK accounting so a buy-sell round trip preserves or increases the trader's starting asset breaks the net-negative assertion.

claims.claim: AssetKind bitmask namespaces keep native, local, foreign, LP, and staking-receipt asset ids distinguishable.
claims.falsification_note: Changing namespace masks or allocation constants so protocol allocations no longer sum cleanly or asset-kind checks collide breaks parameter and allocation assertions.

claims.claim: The asset registry persists forward and reverse Location <-> AssetId mappings so lookup stays bounded and bijective.
claims.falsification_note: Allowing duplicate locations or duplicate asset ids, or failing to persist reverse mappings, breaks duplicate-registration/linking tests.

claims.claim: ActorHot.queue_ticket prevents duplicate live queue entries across repeated enqueue attempts.
claims.falsification_note: Removing same-epoch queue dedup causes repeated enqueue attempts for one actor to produce more than one runnable queue entry.

claims.claim: Staking reward epoch rollover uses a persisted cursor and per-block caps so epoch close remains resumable and bounded.
claims.falsification_note: Removing the pending rollover cursor or processing all buckets in one pass breaks the cross-block rollover resume assertions.

claims.claim: AAA active-cap cleanup stays bounded and economically cheaper to clean than to spam-fill.
claims.falsification_note: Reducing actor creation cost or making sweep cleanup unbounded breaks the runtime cost-ratio regression for zombie spam cleanup.

claims.claim: Governance reward-memory resolution prevents duplicate winning-vote ingestion inside the live reward horizon.
claims.falsification_note: Removing live-horizon resolution memory lets the same item/account resolution be credited twice and breaks duplicate-resolution tests.
